relations.note: Coral restriction narrows tertiary-accent usage within the overall hierarchy.
Chart-specific color roles apply the general palette hierarchy.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Preserve the approved brand color hierarchy across headings and body.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio: blues and golds dominate, tertiary colors are accents/surfaces. Headlines/headings use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b), or White on dark/gradient backgrounds. Body text is Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds, White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height per §1.2).

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep chart colors legible and reserve gold as an emphasis-only signal.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) + Sky Blue (#358CCB) dominate; Sunshine/Gold is strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; placebo bars use gray (chart.color.placebo).

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Distinguish data vs. text-heavy callout surfaces with approved tints.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue at 70% tint (opacity.callout.light_blue_70 = 0.7) for data callouts, and Golden Sand at 30% tint (opacity.callout.golden_sand_30 = 0.3) for text-heavy callouts.

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accent only (never primary CTAs, headlines, or data)
effect.subject: tok_lisraya_color_tertiary_coral

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Constrain Coral to rare accent use and keep it out of email unless briefed.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL] Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Ensure links are consistently styled and legible against their background.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Links are Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b), underlined (body.link.decoration = underline), on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Protect trademark integrity of the logo mark's gold treatment.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a (gold) must be maintained in all instances — in the logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic.
